title: QA Ellipsoid Slicing — Latitude Circles, Meridian Ellipse, Chromogeometric Families
_fixture_purpose: Certifies three types of QA slicing of the WGS84 quantum ellipse: (1) latitude circles with R²=a²d²c_φ/(d²-e²s_φ), (2) meridian ellipses with axis ratio √F/d, (3) chromogeometric slice families C/F/G. Plus 24-fold Pisano longitude bands.

latitude_slices.label: Equator (s_φ=0)
latitude_slices._note: R = a_earth (maximum). The equatorial circle. c_φ=1, denom=d²=12100.

latitude_slices.label: Tropic of Cancer (s_φ≈0.158)
latitude_slices._note: Near quantum lattice point s_φ=C/G=0.1625. Sun's maximum declination.

latitude_slices.label: 45°N (s_φ=1/2)
latitude_slices._note: Spread midpoint. R ≈ a/√2 (adjusted for eccentricity).

latitude_slices.label: Pole (s_φ=1)
latitude_slices._note: Degenerate: circle collapses to a point. c_φ=0 → R=0.

meridian_ellipse._note: Every meridian has the SAME ellipse shape as the whole Earth: axis ratio √F/d = √12019/110. The ellipsoid is self-similar under meridional slicing. e² = 81/12100 = e_QN²/d_QN².
meridian_ellipse._qa_identity: The meridian ellipse IS the shape QN (101,9,110,119) realized as a 2D curve. Slicing the 3D ellipsoid by a meridional plane recovers the generating QN.

chromo_slices.label: Fundamental direction (1,1) — 45° diagonal
chromo_slices._note: F=0 → null direction in red metric (light-like in Minkowski). The 45° line.

chromo_slices.label: Pythagorean (3,4)
chromo_slices._note: C=24=mod. The green quadrance equals the modulus — a chromogeometric resonance.

chromo_slices.label: Earth QN direction (110,9)
chromo_slices._note: The shape QN itself as a chromo slice. C²+F²=G² is the defining identity of the quantum ellipse. Slicing at the Earth's own direction recovers the shape triple.

chromo_slices.label: Orbit QN direction (60,1)
chromo_slices._note: The orbital QN as a chromo slice. G-F=2 always (twin prime structure: 59,61). Shape and orbit chromo slices are harmonically independent.

pisano_bands._note: π(24)=24 → the mod-24 T-operator creates 24 equally-spaced longitude bands, each 15° wide. These correspond to: (a) 24 hours of sidereal rotation, (b) 24 time zones, (c) the 24-fold symmetry of the cosmos orbit. The TIME ZONE SYSTEM is a Pisano-period partition.

qa_connection.self_similarity: Meridional slicing recovers the shape QN — the ellipsoid is self-similar. Latitude slicing produces circles whose radius is a QN expression. The 3D shape encodes its own 2D generator.
qa_connection.three_slice_families: C-constant, F-constant, G-constant curves partition the surface into three families satisfying C²+F²=G² everywhere. These are the green (area), red (Minkowski), and blue (Euclidean) level sets — chromogeometric slicing.
qa_connection.time_zones_are_pisano: The 24 time zones = 24 Pisano bands of 15° each. This is NOT a coincidence — the sidereal day divides naturally into 24 equal parts because the mod-24 cosmos orbit has period 24. Time zones are a Pisano-period partition of the quantum ellipse.
qa_connection.tropic_is_quantum: The Tropic of Cancer (23.44°) falls within 0.34° of the quantum lattice point s_φ = C/G = 1980/12181 (23.78°). The green/blue ratio of the shape QN nearly predicts the axial tilt.
